title: Abigail's Debut Score on Rotten Tomatoes & Early Reviews Are Scary Good
text: The film currently sits at an excellent 85 percent Fresh on review aggregator Rotten Tomatoes. Critics have been largely positive about the film. Critics praise Abigail for being extremely bloody, fun, humorous and fresh; setting the bar high as the most enjoyable horror movie of the year. Carrying off well-worn vampire tropes with a balletic flourish, Abigail dances around the familiarity of its premise with a game cast and slick style. The young actress portraying the vampire ballerina, Alisha Weir, shines, effortlessly switching from terrified to savage, and delivers a commanding performance. Despite the strong critical praise, Abigail is a little silly, a little scary, and a lot of fun--just be prepared for a story that takes its time getting going and might go on a little too long. The film is a modern reimagining of Universal's 1936 horror film Dracula's Daughter and marks a successful entry in the vampire subgenre, standing out among recent vampire movies.
